DMS represents the world’s leading IT brands and specializes in Systems Integration and Customized Software Solutions for Sri Lankan and overseas markets. USA, the world leader in CRMs (Cash Recycling Machines) and ATMs (Automated Teller Machines) and while having implemented the following pioneering Projects, DMS is the market leader supporting an installed base of over 2000 CRMs and ATMs in Sri Lanka and the Maldives. Introduced ATMs to the Maldives in 1999 First CRMs in Sri Lanka and in the Maldives First Branch and Networked Bulk Cash Depositors in Asia Pacific. First Envelope-less Cash Acceptance ATMs in Asia Pacific. First Envelope-less Bulk Cheque Deposit ATMs in Asia Pacific. First Networked Foreign Currency Conversion ATMs which accept foreign currency and dispense Sri Lanka Currency and Coins. The following vacancies now exist for candidates living in and around Matara and Tangalle areas to provide field service and support for ATMs and Cash Recyclers in this area. Engineering Service Executives for Matara and Tangalle areas (Male) Career opportunities exist for disciplined, motivated, self-reliant persons in Field Engineering Services who possess a positive attitude and can work under minimum supervision after initial training. Candidates should: - Be under 27 years of age. Have as a minimum a National Diploma in Engineering Sciences (NDES) or HNDE or NDT or similar qualification in Electronics Engineering or Mechatronics Engineering from a recognized institution. Have knowledge of PC hardware and Windows software. Selected candidates will be required to service and maintain ATMs & Cash Recyclers installed at various banks in the Matara and Tangalle areas. Candidates should reside in the Matara or Tangalle Town or in close proximity and have a motorcycle or scooter together with the relevant riding license. Successful candidates who perform to the Company's expectations have a career path with promotional prospects to Senior ATM Service Engineers, Team Leaders, Group Managers, Department Managers and beyond. text_full: SriLankan Airlines Metrologist Vacancy Description SriLankan Airlines Ltd, the national carrier of Sri Lanka operates with a network of destinations throughout Asia, the Middle East, Europe and Australia. The airlines has a strong presence in Maldives and Southern India. SriLankan is a member of the oneworld alliance. The incumbent is responsible to execute assigned operational functions of the calibration laboratory of SriLankan Engineering by fulfilling the regulatory and organizational requirements and executing its functions in an efficient and timely manner to the satisfaction of its internal/external customers and to take over the assigned responsibilities in the absence of Senior Metrologist. Job Accountabilities To implement, design calibration systems, procedures, and methods according to Calibration Laboratory Manager’s requirements to extend laboratory’s scope of accreditation To implement, review and validate software used in calibration laboratory processes to ensure minimum turnaround time and improved accuracy of calibration results To execute internal and external quality assurance plans to meet regulatory and organizational requirements Conduct technical and quality audits of the laboratory to verify the traceability of measurement results and compliance with published standards and guidelines to ensure that the laboratory processes are in line with the regulatory and organizational requirements Maintain existing calibration systems and procedures based on the principles of measurement science, technical analysis of measurement problems and precision requirements. Periodic review of documents and records required for efficient execution of laboratory functions. Requirements Bachelor of Engineering (1) in Aircraft/Aerospace/Mechanical/Mechatronics/ Electrical and/or Electronic) or Bachelor of Science in Physical/Applied Sciences with two years of relevant post qualifying experience. OR Full professional qualification equivalent to NVQ level 7(2) in the field of aircraft/Aerospace/Mechanical/Mechatronics/ Electrical and/or Electronic), with three years of relevant post-qualifying experience. Note: 1. Qualification shall be awarded through an educational institute recognized by the University Grants Commission of Sri Lanka. 2. Qualification shall be awarded through an educational institute recognized by the Tertiary and Vocational Education Commission of Sri Lanka. Age to be not more than 50 years as at 07th March 2026 which is the closing date. Be a SriLankan Citizen. Employment will be offered on fixed term contract. Influencing will be a reflection of unsuitability. We are an equal opportunity Organization. Please note that we will correspond with you within a month of closing the advertisement in the event your application is shortlisted for consideration.
